content: Never use 'guarantee', 'guaranteed', 'ensures', or 'promise' when describing customer outcomes, ROI, or results. If there is a citable customer result, phrase it as "customers have reported..." and cite the published source — never assert it as a certainty.
Avoid 'risk-free', 'guaranteed results', or 'zero-risk' framing anywhere in partner content, including headlines, CTAs, and image captions.

Do not use unqualified superlatives about KLZ or the joint solution: 'no other platform comes close', 'best-in-class', 'unmatched', 'unrivaled', '#1', "world's best". Describe what the solution does concretely instead (e.g. 'automates document-heavy workflows across finance, legal, and operations').
'Industry-leading' and 'category-defining' require a cited third-party source (analyst report, published benchmark); used without one, they count as unapproved superlatives.

Any numeric claim about outcomes (percentages, multipliers like '10x', dollar savings, time saved) requires either a published source URL or written KLZ approval attached to the submission. Flag any percent / multiplier / dollar-amount pattern that has no adjacent citation.
Do not reference 'Agent Marketplace', its Q4 2026 target date, or any KLZ product/feature not yet publicly announced. Roadmap communications come from KLZ only.

Internal codenames are denylisted from partner content: 'Project Lighthouse', 'Orchestrate 2.0', 'Agent Marketplace'. If a partner asset references an internal codename, remove it and describe only shipped, generally-available capabilities.
Any forward-looking date attached to a KLZ capability ('launching in Q_ 20__', 'coming soon') is a roadmap disclosure unless the feature already has a public KLZ announcement to cite.

Approved KLZ spokespeople for partner content: Dana Whitfield (VP Partnerships) and Sam Okafor (Head of Ecosystem). A quote attributed to any other KLZ employee — including executives — requires written, piece-specific approval from KLZ Partner Marketing before it can be published.

Approved quote — Dana Whitfield, VP Partnerships, KLZ: "We built KLZ Orchestrate so builders like our partners can ship agentic workflows without reinventing infrastructure." Partners may use this quote verbatim with attribution.

Approved quote — Sam Okafor, Head of Ecosystem, KLZ: "Our best integrations come from partners who understand their customers' workflows better than we ever could." Partners may use this quote verbatim with attribution.
'Powered by KLZ Orchestrate' badge is Elite-tier only. Select-tier partners must use the 'KLZ Select Partner' designation instead. Registered-tier partners may not display any KLZ badge or lockup.
Tier badge map: Elite -> "Powered by KLZ Orchestrate"; Select -> "KLZ Select Partner"; Registered -> no badge (a plain-text mention of a 'KLZ integration' is permitted; no lockup or badge graphic).
KLZ Orchestrate is the agentic orchestration layer that lets partners connect Claude-native agents to their own systems of record — without rebuilding their data infrastructure first.
KLZ Orchestrate automates document-heavy workflows across finance, legal, and operations by combining Claude's reasoning with your existing systems of record.

KLZ Orchestrate's claim-review agent checks partner-submitted content against KLZ's approved messaging corpus and brand rules, and returns categorized findings with a recommended change for each. It does not rewrite or generate replacement marketing copy.

In KLZ's Q2 2026 customer survey (published at klz.com/reports/q2-2026), joint customers reported a median 35% reduction in manual workflow processing time across a sample of 40 partner deployments. Results vary by environment and integration depth.

KLZ's published benchmark (klz.com/benchmarks/agentic-review-2026) found that partner-submitted content using KLZ Orchestrate's review agent reached publish-ready status in a median of 1.4 review cycles, down from 3.2 cycles under manual review.

KLZ's partner program is built on three tiers — Registered, Select, and Elite — each unlocking deeper co-marketing, technical enablement, and joint go-to-market support as partners demonstrate integration depth and customer outcomes.

KLZ Orchestrate never auto-sends partner or customer communications. Every agent-drafted action — an email, an export, an outreach message — lands in a pending state for a human to review, edit, or approve before it goes out.

Partner content must not include personal data about individuals outside the approved-spokesperson context: email addresses, Social Security Numbers, phone numbers, or payment-card numbers. Redact or remove any such data before publishing, and escalate to KLZ Partner Marketing if a submission appears to contain it.
